title: Authentication required
detail: Valid authentication credentials are required to access this resource.
reason: The access token included in this request is either missing, expired, or has been revoked. Tokens issued by this API expire after 1 hour. If the token was recently working, it most likely expired - use the refresh token flow rather than asking the user to log in again.

limitations.detail: The presented access token cannot be used. If you have a refresh token, exchange it for a new access token at the token endpoint. Refresh tokens are valid for 30 days.

next_actions.title: Refresh the access token
next_actions.description: Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token. This is a silent operation that does not require user interaction if a refresh token is available. The response includes both a new access token (1 hour TTL) and a new refresh token (30 day TTL). If the refresh token is also expired, this will return 401 and the user must re-authenticate from scratch via the authorize endpoint.

next_actions.title: Start new authentication
next_actions.description: Initiates a full OAuth2 authorization flow. This requires user interaction - the user will need to log in and grant permissions. Only use this if the refresh token flow fails or no refresh token is available. Opens in the user's browser.

agent_guidance: Try the refresh_token action first - it does not require user interaction and is the preferred recovery path.
Only fall back to the full authorize flow if the refresh fails. In that case, explain to the user that their session expired and they need to log in again.
Do not display or log any tokens, refresh tokens, or credentials in plain text.
After obtaining a new token, retry the original request that triggered this 401 - do not ask the user to repeat their action.
